# YOUR CODE HERE
import sys
import math

def distance(p1, p2):
    # Calculate Euclidean distance between two points on a regular pentagon
    # Points are labeled A, B, C, D, E in clockwise order
    # Assume regular pentagon is inscribed in a unit circle
    points = {
        'A': (1, 0),
        'B': (math.cos(72 * math.pi / 180), math.sin(72 * math.pi / 180)),
        'C': (math.cos(144 * math.pi / 180), math.sin(144 * math.pi / 180)),
        'D': (math.cos(216 * math.pi / 180), math.sin(216 * math.pi / 180)),
        'E': (math.cos(288 * math.pi / 180), math.sin(288 * math.pi / 180))
    }
    return math.hypot(points[p1][0] - points[p2][0], points[p1][1] - points[p2][1])

def main():
    # Read the two lines of input
    line1 = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
    line2 = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
    
    # Extract the points
    s1, s2 = line1[0], line1[1]
    t1, t2 = line2[0], line2[1]
    
    d1 = distance(s1, s2)
    d2 = distance(t1, t2)
    print("Yes" if abs(d1 - d2) < 1e-9 else "No")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
